object_under_evaluation: integrity of reviewed-candidate adoption on runtime-to-memo distillation paths
claim_boundary: On the currently inspected public memo surfaces, reviewed-candidate adoption now supports one bounded claim/pattern/bridge path from runtime mapping through review anchor, live receipt publication, and object-facing recall without silently upgrading the bridge leg into final lift or settled graph truth.
limitations: This report does not prove reviewed-candidate promotion quality.
This report does not prove contradiction handling quality.
This report does not prove confirmed memo-surviving-event writeback quality.
This report does not prove KAG-lift completion or bridge promotion.
This report does not prove generic live memory-ledger behavior.
strongest_adoption_signal: The memo layer now publishes explicit reviewed-candidate receipts for claim, pattern, and bridge paths, and each receipt ties runtime_surface, adopted object, review anchor, and recall catalog visibility together on one public lane.
strongest_adoption_risk: The bridge leg remains candidate-shaped and must still be read as adopted memo visibility only, not as bridge promotion, KAG lift completion, or downstream graph truth.

case_notes.candidate_note: The claim leg keeps explicit distillation candidate posture through the runtime mapping and the published reviewed-candidate receipt.
case_notes.review_note: The receipt names the reviewed validation-driven-remediation anchor while preserving the adopted claim's confirmed lifecycle and contradiction-aware recall posture.
case_notes.receipt_note: The live receipt log and tracked fixture now publish this claim as a reviewed-candidate adoption event with runtime_surface and writeback anchor refs.
case_notes.recall_note: The adopted claim remains inspectable downstream with explicit review state, provenance, and current-recall posture.

case_notes.candidate_note: The pattern leg now has one public reviewed-candidate receipt that isolates the adoption path instead of leaving the pattern leg implied by adjacent recurrence evidence alone.
case_notes.review_note: The receipt keeps the rerun review anchor explicit and preserves the adopted pattern's bounded recurrence posture without widening it into general technique canon.
case_notes.receipt_note: The live receipt log and tracked fixture now publish the pattern leg as a reviewed-candidate adoption event with aligned runtime_surface and adopted source-path refs.
case_notes.recall_note: The pattern remains recallable through the object-facing catalog, capsules, and sections with lifecycle posture intact.

case_notes.candidate_note: The bridge leg remains visibly candidate-shaped while now also carrying one explicit reviewed-candidate receipt that separates adoption from later lift stories.
case_notes.review_note: The receipt points back to the memo-side bridge contract anchor and preserves the bridge object's proposed review_state and candidate-only KAG posture.
case_notes.receipt_note: The live receipt log and tracked fixture now publish the bridge leg as a reviewed-candidate adoption event instead of leaving the bridge path receipt-silent.
case_notes.recall_note: Recall posture stays honest because the bridge remains inspectable as a proposed candidate on memo surfaces rather than being flattened into finalized bridge truth.
